message:
test(package_manager): tighten reconcile assertion to verify the right entry is pruned

Follow-up on 0360800d. The reconcile test's `pruned[0]` assertion
only checked `.identity.starts_with("local:")` — both pkg1 and pkg2
are local packages in the test fixture, so the assertion would have
passed even if reconcile had incorrectly pruned pkg1 instead of
pkg2. A later assertion catches that via `after.entries.iter().any(|e| e.source == "./pkg1")`,
so the test as a whole still fails on the wrong-entry case — but the
specific failure mode would be confusing.

Tighten to `assert_eq!(pruned[0].source, "./pkg2")` so the
regression surfaces at the right line with a clear reason. Also
tighten the identity check from `.starts_with("local:")` to
`.ends_with("pkg2")` so we positively identify pkg2 specifically.

No behavior change; test diagnostics quality only.
